title: The Epistle of Othea
summary: The checked-in CME00015 XML copies the University of Michigan CME encoding of the title page and printed pp. 1-114 of George F. Warner's 1904 edition of Stephen Scrope's Middle English Epistle of Othea. Warner transcribed the incomplete Longleat House MS 253, ff. 2r-75v, and collated French readings against British Library Harley MS 4431. The conceptual translation has one hundred chapters, but Longleat has lost a leaf after f. 9v and a quire after f. 34v. Misty Schieberle's 2020 edition instead uses St John's College MS H.5 as base and collates Morgan MS M.775 and Longleat, with Bodleian Laud Misc. 570 among the French comparison sources. Complete PDFs of Warner and Schieberle are cached; provider-complete selected-image bundles are cached for Morgan and Bodleian, and a complete 001r-399v published leaf sequence is cached privately for Harley. No complete public physical-object facsimile was found for Longleat, St John's, Morgan, or Bodleian.
review_notes: Scrope's Middle English translation, Christine's French source work, the three English witnesses, the French comparison witnesses, Warner and Schieberle's editions, image surrogates, the U-M encoding, and the repository artifact are distinct layers.
The direct French exemplar used by Scrope has not survived. Bodleian Laud Misc. 570 is a sister manuscript probably copied from the same lost French source, not a demonstrated physical exemplar.
Warner's edition follows incomplete Longleat MS 253 and uses Harley MS 4431 as French comparison H; it does not transcribe Harley as its English base.
Schieberle's edition uses St John's H.5 as base and compares Morgan M.775 and Longleat 253; it is collateral scholarship, not the source of CME00015.

Completeness claims for cached image bundles are provider-inventory claims and are qualified against the physical object: Morgan and Bodleian expose selected subsets; Harley exposes the complete published 001r-399v leaf sequence but no covers, boards, spine, or binding views.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for CME00015
entities.description: The XML artifact consumed by this repository's conversion and publication workflow, supplied through the Kyvero-Press/CME git submodule.
notes: IDG/@ID, BIBNO, and VID all identify the file as CME00015.
TEMPHEAD records a structural review dated 15 June 2016.

entities.label: University of Michigan Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse item CME00015
entities.description: A digital edition-level selection preserving Warner's title page, printed page breaks, Longleat folio milestones, verse lines, prose, editorial notes, chapter divisions, commentary, allegorical interpretations, and lacunae.

date_statements.label: repository XML structural review
date_statements.notes: This dates a revision of the serialization, not the first online publication of the U-M item.

notes: The current U-M item labels the material Public Domain and applies No Copyright-United States.
The encoding excludes Warner's introduction, errata, plates as independent objects, glossary, and index.

entities.label: Warner, The Epistle of Othea to Hector (1904)
alternate_labels: The Epistle of Othea to Hector or The Boke of Knyghthode
entities.description: George F. Warner's first printed edition of Scrope's English text, based on Longleat House MS 253 and furnished with an introduction, French comparison readings from Harley MS 4431, glossary, and index.

bibliographic.citation: Christine de Pizan, The Epistle of Othea to Hector or The Boke of Knyghthode, translated from the French by Stephen Scrope, edited from a manuscript in the library of the Marquis of Bath by George F. Warner (London: J. B. Nichols and Sons, 1904).
bibliographic.title: The Epistle of Othea to Hector or The Boke of Knyghthode

notes: The English work occupies printed pp. 1-114; CME00015 encodes that text and the title page.
Warner recognized the missing Longleat leaf and quire and used brackets and notes for comparison or supplied matter rather than silently claiming a complete base witness.

entities.label: Internet Archive scan of Warner's 1904 Epistle of Othea
entities.description: A complete downloadable PDF scan of a University of California copy of Warner's edition.

entities.label: Longleat House MS 253
entities.description: A mid-fifteenth-century English parchment codex of 95 folios containing Scrope's Epistle on ff. 2r-75v and The Tree of Love on ff. 76r-95v. The Epistle lacks a leaf after f. 9v and a quire after f. 34v and retains unfilled spaces for rubrics, initials, Latin quotations, and miniatures.

holding.notes: The current public access page supplies enquiry, image-request, and reading-room procedures but no item-level record or public facsimile.

physical_description.format: Codex in nineteenth-century dark brown leather binding
notes: The present fol. 1 is an old parchment cover carrying a title.
The work's documentary, nonprofessional hand and copying errors do not support Warner's historical suggestion that this was the original Fastolf copy.

entities.label: St John's College, Cambridge, MS H.5
entities.description: The most complete of the three surviving English witnesses and the base manuscript for Schieberle 2020. A professional mid-fifteenth-century parchment copy, probably written by Ricardus Franciscus, with a complete visual program and dedication to Humphrey Stafford; one leaf is missing after f. 55v.

physical_description.format: Illuminated codex containing the Epistle, with later flyleaf additions
notes: Six illuminations survive in a complete planned decorative program.

entities.label: Morgan Library & Museum, MS M.775
entities.description: A professional mid-fifteenth-century English parchment composite of 320 folios. Scrope's acephalous Epistle occupies ff. 200r-274v, is addressed to an unnamed princess, and was later appropriated by Sir John Astley; some miniatures are missing or removed.

date_statements.label: manuscript production
date_statements.value: Third or fourth quarter of the fifteenth century

physical_description.format: Composite codex in sixteenth-century stamped calf binding

entities.label: Morgan M.775 public selected-image set
entities.description: The complete set of 14 low-resolution leaf images publicly exposed by the Morgan thumbnail viewer; only ff. 200r, 202r, 204r, and 274r fall within the Epistle.
entities.reproduction_format: Fourteen selected color JPEGs bundled with an exact URL inventory
notes: This is not a complete physical-object facsimile.

entities.label: Bodleian Library, MS Laud Misc. 570
entities.description: A French parchment manuscript associated with Sir John Fastolf and the Master of Sir John Fastolf. Christine's Epistre Othea occupies ff. 24r-92v. It is textually close to Scrope's lost French source but is not established as his direct physical exemplar.

notes: Digital Bodleian describes French origin and Middle French language and associates the illumination with the Master of Sir John Fastolf.

entities.label: Digital Bodleian selected images for Laud Misc. 570
entities.description: The complete 30-canvas IIIF Presentation inventory published for a 93-folio manuscript, derived largely from older 35mm photography and including duplicate whole-page and detail frames.
entities.reproduction_format: Thirty selected high-resolution color IIIF images
notes: This is complete for the provider's selected IIIF inventory, not for the physical manuscript.

entities.label: British Library, Harley MS 4431
entities.description: Christine de Pizan's lavish presentation collection for Queen Isabeau, produced in Paris around 1413-1414 and now bound in two volumes. The French Epistre Othea occupies ff. 95r-141v and is illustrated by 101 miniatures. Warner uses this witness as French comparison H.

physical_description.extent: A complete published sequence numbered 001r-399v; the project describes 398 folios and thirty works
physical_description.format: Lavishly illuminated composite collection, now bound as two volumes
notes: The Edinburgh project reports 132 miniatures across the manuscript.

entities.label: Edinburgh Christine de Pizan Project complete published leaf sequence for Harley MS 4431
entities.description: The provider's complete published manuscript-image sequence, comprising 798 direct JPEGs from 001r through 399v. It covers every leaf side in the project's inventory but offers no covers, boards, spine, pastedowns, or separate binding views.
entities.reproduction_format: 798 color JPEG leaf images at the single provider-offered resolution
notes: The project is archival and no longer maintained.
Image copyright is marked © 2005 The British Library, all rights reserved.

entities.label: Schieberle, Advice for Princes in Middle English Translation (2020)
entities.description: Misty Schieberle's modern edition of Scrope's Epistle, based on St John's H.5 and collated against Morgan M.775 and Longleat 253, published with extensive introduction and apparatus.

bibliographic.citation: Christine de Pizan, Advice for Princes in Middle English Translation: Stephen Scrope's The Epistle of Othea and the Anonymous Lytle Bibell of Knyghthod, edited by Misty Schieberle (Kalamazoo: Medieval Institute Publications, 2020).
bibliographic.title: Advice for Princes in Middle English Translation: Stephen Scrope's The Epistle of Othea and the Anonymous Lytle Bibell of Knyghthod

entities.label: METS downloadable PDF of Schieberle 2020
entities.description: A complete 492-page publisher PDF of the modern edition; Scrope's Epistle occupies printed pp. 33-134.

entities.label: Christine de Pizan's French Epistre Othea
entities.description: The French source work composed around 1400, designed as one hundred texte, glose, and allegorie chapters. Scrope's Middle English Epistle is a translation of this work; no surviving manuscript is equated with the abstract source work or with Scrope's lost exemplar.

assertion.notes: Identifiers, collection path, stable UMDL handle, and edition boundary align; a current downloadable U-M source package was not available for byte comparison.

work_portion.label: Christine de Pizan, Epistre Othea in Harley MS 4431
notes: Warner uses this French manuscript as siglum H for comparison and supplied readings; it is not the base witness for the Middle English text.
The provider describes this as a complete set of manuscript images and exposes 798 leaf-side JPEGs, 001r through 399v; the gallery offers no covers, boards, spine, or binding views.

The legacy project footer states: All manuscript images © 2005 The British Library; All rights reserved. Current University of Edinburgh website terms permit private-purpose viewing and downloading but require permission for storage, reproduction, or transmission for other purposes. Retain this cache only for private internal research; do not redistribute or publish images without permission.
The provider offers one direct JPEG profile rather than an IIIF Image API. Images are retained at the exact offered dimensions without upscaling; sample 095r is 1229 by 1536 pixels.
ZIP contains the normalized source URL list, an exact-source inventory, and all 798 images in the verified full-object list.
Effective IIIF image request profile(s): "provider_resource"; format: jpg.
The provider offers one direct JPEG profile rather than an IIIF sizing service; sample 095r is 1229 x 1536 pixels, so the cache uses exact offered files without upscaling.

The project footer marks images © 2005 The British Library, all rights reserved. University web terms permit private-purpose downloading but not storage or reproduction for other purposes without permission.

rights.component: Warner's 1904 edition, including the Middle English transcription and historical editorial matter
rights.basis: The edition was published in 1904; the Internet Archive item marks it NOT_IN_COPYRIGHT and the current U-M item applies No Copyright-United States. This is not a worldwide rights statement.

rights.jurisdiction: Provider statement and intended reuse jurisdiction
rights.basis: The item is publicly downloadable and marked NOT_IN_COPYRIGHT. Internet Archive provides the file but does not warrant every downstream use; attribution and independent jurisdiction review remain prudent.

rights.component: U-M electronic transcription, structure, and markup
rights.basis: The current item applies No Copyright-United States to the material, but the inspected rights badge does not separately analyze any protectable electronic markup or production metadata.

rights.component: Checked-in XML serialization and repository-specific modifications
rights.jurisdiction: Repository terms and intended reuse jurisdiction
rights.basis: The file contains public-domain source text but no embedded item-level license independently covering its serialization, markup, or later modifications.

rights.component: Schieberle's modern introduction, edition, apparatus, and publisher PDF
rights.jurisdiction: Provider terms and applicable copyright jurisdictions
rights.basis: METS states that digital editions are for individual use and instructs users to contact the director to reprint digital text. Its separate XML offer is CC BY-NC-SA 4.0, but that does not license the cached publisher PDF as a whole.

rights.jurisdiction: Provider terms and intended reuse jurisdiction
rights.basis: Morgan terms permit images to be printed or downloaded for personal, educational, noncommercial use when unaltered and credited; commercial use, publication, and high-resolution supply require authorization. The permission is use-specific and is not a public-domain statement.

rights.jurisdiction: Creative Commons license and intended reuse jurisdiction
rights.basis: The item manifest attributes Photo © Bodleian Libraries and applies CC BY-NC 4.0. Digital Bodleian terms permit download and noncommercial reuse with attribution; commercial uses require separate contact.

rights.component: Edinburgh project's British Library manuscript JPEGs
rights.jurisdiction: Provider terms and intended reuse jurisdiction
rights.basis: Every project page states 'All manuscript images © 2005 The British Library' and 'All rights reserved.' The project links no special reuse license. Current University of Edinburgh website terms expressly permit private-purpose viewing and downloading, while storage, reproduction, or transmission for other purposes requires permission. The private source cache is therefore not distributable or publishable.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Scrope's Epistle on Longleat ff. 2r-75v within a 95-folio codex that continues with The Tree of Love
editorial_practices.representation: The complete surviving Epistle is printed on pp. 1-114 as the edition's main text; the adjacent work is excluded
editorial_practices.description: Warner makes one manuscript work the edition boundary without treating the whole codex as one text.

editorial_practices.source_feature: An incomplete English base with blank spaces, copying errors, a missing leaf, and a missing quire
editorial_practices.representation: Warner marks losses, compares the French Harley MS 4431 as H, supplies some Latin or omitted matter in brackets, and records comparison readings in notes
editorial_practices.description: The apparatus helps interpret or supplement Longleat but must not be mistaken for text physically present in that witness.
editorial_practices.scope: English text and notes, printed pp. 1-114

editorial_practices.source_feature: Three independent, nonautograph mid-fifteenth-century English witnesses with different dedications, losses, and readings
editorial_practices.representation: St John's H.5 (S) supplies the base text; Morgan M.775 (M) and Longleat 253 (L) supply variants and missing or comparative readings, with French manuscripts used to assess the source tradition
editorial_practices.description: The modern edition reconstructs a fuller Scrope text than Warner's single incomplete Longleat base while documenting witness differences.
editorial_practices.scope: The Epistle of Othea, printed pp. 33-134 and apparatus

editorial_practices.source_feature: Warner's complete 1904 volume with title matter, introduction, plates, errata, English text, glossary, and index
editorial_practices.representation: The title page and English work on printed pp. 1-114 are encoded; Warner's introduction, errata, glossary, index, and nontextual plate objects are excluded
editorial_practices.description: CME00015 is an edition-level work selection, not a complete digital facsimile or transcription of the 1904 volume.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Warner's typographic chapter sequence with recurring texte, glose, and allegorie units, plus Longleat losses
editorial_practices.representation: DIV2 chapter structures and DIV3 commentary/allegorical interpretation structures were checked and revised in 2016; page breaks, folio milestones, notes, verse lines, and lacunae remain explicit
editorial_practices.description: The XML makes recurring semantic units computationally visible but its element counts describe the surviving edition representation, not a new canonical extent.

evidence.citation: General Corpus / CME source XML, CME/source/CME_phase_3/CME00015.xml.
evidence.locator: TEMPHEAD/REVDESCR; EEBO/IDG; title page; TEXT/BODY
evidence.summary: Identifies CME00015 through IDG/@ID, BIBNO, and VID; records the 15 June 2016 structural review; and encodes Warner's title page, Fastolf preface, incomplete chapter sequence, notes, page breaks, folio milestones, commentary, allegory, and lacunae.

evidence.citation: Structural XPath inspection of CME00015.xml.
evidence.locator: Counts of DIV1, numbered and unnumbered DIV2, DIV3 types, P, L excluding NOTE ancestors, NOTE, PB, and MILESTONE
evidence.summary: Finds 3 DIV1 containers, 89 numbered chapters, one unnumbered chapter-49 fragment, one lacuna placeholder, 90 commentary and 88 allegorical DIV3 elements, 481 non-note verse lines, 188 BODY paragraphs, 492 BODY notes, 115 page breaks, and 72 milestones.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse, item CME00015.
evidence.locator: Stable item identity, publication statement, and current rights badge
evidence.summary: Responsible digital-collection evidence for the CME identifier, Warner 1904 bibliographic source, Public Domain label, and No Copyright-United States statement.

evidence.citation: Christine de Pizan, The Epistle of Othea to Hector or The Boke of Knyghthode, translated by Stephen Scrope, edited by George F. Warner (London: J. B. Nichols and Sons, 1904).
evidence.locator: Title page; introduction pp. i-xlvii; English text pp. 1-114; glossary and index
evidence.summary: Direct edition evidence for title, Christine/Scrope/Warner responsibilities, Fastolf dedication, Longleat base, manuscript losses, approximate translation date, first printing, and Warner's Harley H comparison.

evidence.citation: Visual inspection and text extraction of the complete Internet Archive PDF for Warner 1904.
evidence.locator: Cached PDF title matter, introduction, pp. 1-114, glossary, index, and final leaves
evidence.summary: Confirms complete-volume coverage, exact work page range, title-page wording, Longleat and Harley plates, French H apparatus, bracketed supplies, gaps, and the edition material omitted from CME00015.

evidence.citation: Christine de Pizan, Advice for Princes in Middle English Translation: Stephen Scrope's The Epistle of Othea and the Anonymous Lytle Bibell of Knyghthod, edited by Misty Schieberle (Kalamazoo: Medieval Institute Publications, 2020).
evidence.locator: Introduction; Epistle text printed pp. 33-134; witness and textual notes
evidence.summary: Current scholarly evidence for Scrope's date and dedications, all three English witnesses, S-base/M-and-L collation, Longleat losses, French BI-family source relations, genre, form, chapter design, and the nonidentity of Laud Misc. 570 with a demonstrated direct exemplar.

evidence.citation: Dated physical-surface coverage check for the Harley MS 4431 Edinburgh image sequence.
evidence.summary: The project inventory exhaustively runs from leaf 001r through 399v and claims a complete high-resolution image set, but it publishes no exterior binding surfaces. No public covers, boards, spine, pastedowns, or separate binding images were found on 11 July 2026; British Library digitisation services provide a request route.

open_questions.question: Can a complete color physical-object surrogate of Longleat House MS 253 be obtained and retained locally?
next_steps: Ask the Longleat House Collections team whether complete existing photography or microfilm of MS 253 is available.
Request the binding, spine, boards or covers, pastedowns, endleaves, blanks, all 95 folios, and any inserted or detached surfaces, not only Othea ff. 2r-75v.
Map Scrope's work separately and confirm retention and reuse terms before caching supplied files.

open_questions.question: Can a complete color physical-object surrogate of St John's College MS H.5 be obtained and retained locally?
next_steps: Email St John's Special Collections for a complete private research copy or an inventory of existing photography.
Request every physical surface, including binding, endleaves, blanks, all manuscript leaves, the missing-leaf context after f. 55v, and later flyleaf additions.
Confirm written retention and reuse terms before caching any supplied files.

open_questions.question: Can a complete color physical-object surrogate of Morgan MS M.775 replace the selected-image bundle?
next_steps: Ask Morgan Imaging and Rights whether complete existing photography can be supplied for private research.
Request binding, spine, boards, pastedowns, endleaves, blanks, all 320 folios, detached or removed-element documentation, and color targets where offered.
Map the Othea portion separately to ff. 200r-274v and preserve exact image locators and terms.
notes: The retained 14-image bundle is complete only for the public selected-image inventory.

open_questions.question: Can a complete color physical-object surrogate of Bodleian MS. Laud Misc. 570 replace the selected IIIF bundle?
next_steps: Ask Bodleian Special Collections mediated copying or Imaging Services about complete existing photography.
Request binding, spine, boards, pastedowns, endleaves, blanks, all 93 folios, and color targets where offered.
Map the French Othea separately to ff. 24r-92v and preserve item-level CC and service terms.
notes: The retained 30-canvas bundle is complete only for the provider's selected IIIF inventory.

open_questions.question: Can the complete published 001r-399v Harley sequence be augmented with every omitted physical-object surface?
next_steps: Ask British Library Imaging Services whether current or legacy photographs exist for both bindings, spines, boards, pastedowns, endleaves, blanks not represented by folio numbering, and color or scale targets.
Keep the private Edinburgh sequence as complete for the provider-published leaf inventory only.
Obtain written permission before any redistribution or publication of the cached JPEGs.

open_questions.question: What keying method, correction history, source copy, and item-level markup rights apply to the U-M CME00015 encoding?
next_steps: Request production metadata and any downloadable source package from the U-M Digital Collections team.
Clarify whether the current No Copyright-United States badge is intended to cover only source material or also any protectable encoding layer.
Compare a stratified sample of XML readings and notes against the cached Warner PDF.
